text.title: Cross-Team Estimation Normalisation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Cross-Team Estimation Normalisation application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Program rollup uses throughput (items per week) and cycle-time. Summing story points across teams = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Story points are team-relative; summing them is fiction.

rule.statement: Per-team throughput rows carry team_id, period (ISO week), items_completed (int), cycle_time_median (days), source path.
Program forecast carries per-team confidence bands (e.g. team A: 8-12 items/wk).
Every report includes the non-comparability caveat. Skipping the caveat = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without the caveat, the steering committee draws wrong conclusions.

rule.statement: Cross-team forecasts require a quarterly calibration session. Forecasts without recent calibration = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Drift between teams accumulates without periodic re-calibration.

rule.statement: Each rollup carries ISO week + semver. Prior rollups append-only.
Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
